sections.paragraph:
adj. f. דַּקָּה (from the root דָּקַק)-

(1) beaten small, fine, minute, pr. used of dust. Isa 29:5, אָבָק דַּק “fine dust.” Lev 16:12, hence subst. something small or fine, dust, particle, Exo 16:14 Isa 40:15.

(2) slender, thin, used of slender, thin hair, Lev 13:30 of lean kine and thin ears of corn, Gen 41:3, seq.; of a man too much emaciated, or having a withered limb, Lev 21:20 light, gentle, of a gale, 1Ki 19:12.
